artifacts.content:
From: jordan.a.miller06@gmail.com
To: erin.miller@email.com
Subject: Stuff I should probably tell you
Date: 2025-09-22

Mom,

I’ve been meaning to write this out because it’s easier than trying to blurt it in the kitchen while Max is yelling about Minecraft.

First, I’m okay, but the panic attack I had during 6th‑hour AP Physics on 9/18 last year still hangs over me. Remember when they called you because I was in the nurse’s office shaking and could barely breathe? Since then I’ve been seeing Ms. Ortiz every other Tuesday in Room 214, and it actually helps. She knows about the late‑night arguments over Dad’s gambling and the whole “maybe selling the house on Lakeview Terrace” thing. I still get tight in the chest when I hear you guys fighting about money.

You also know about when I fainted during the home soccer game on September 18, 2024, but I don’t think I ever said how scared I was afterward. Dr. Werner at the UW Health Behavioral Health Clinic wants me to keep coming every other Tuesday afternoon through at least December 2026 and stay on the sertraline 50 mg that she and the UW Health primary care doctor put me on after that. I’m fine with that, just wanted you looped in.

On top of that, I’m still trying to forget the West Towne Mall thing from March 2024 when Madison PD questioned me after Tyler got caught shoplifting those $120 headphones at Best Buy. I wasn’t charged, but Coach Reynolds put me on a one‑week probation from varsity and I missed three days of track. I hate that it’s still in the incident report.

Anyway, I’m not trying to dump this on you, just didn’t want it all stuck in my head. We can talk whenever you’re not grading.

Love,
Jordan

metadata.subject: Stuff I should probably tell you

artifacts.content:
From: jordan.a.miller06@gmail.com
To: alex@lakesidebrew.com
Subject: Fall schedule + hours during season
Date: 2024-08-05

Hey Alex,

Hope you’re surviving the summer rush. I wanted to check in about my schedule once school and soccer start back up.

Last October when I was working 22–25 hours a week, my AP Physics grade with Mr. Larson at Westview dropped from an A– to a C+. I was pulling all‑nighters and literally nodding off in class and even during homeroom. My counselor, Ms. Ramirez, actually wrote a behavior note because I kept falling asleep. She and I talked and agreed I should cap my Lakeside Brew hours at around 15 per week during soccer season so I’m not a zombie.

I’m still planning on doing mostly evening shifts and some weekends. I can definitely help cover busy nights when there’s a game the next day instead of that night. I just can’t repeat last October where I’d work until close, then try to study, then drag myself to early‑morning weight room and late practices.

Also, minor health thing: a while back I started using this high‑caffeine pre‑workout powder from the GNC at West Towne Mall to get through 5 a.m. shifts and late practices. In March 2025 my labs with Dr. Priya Shah at UW Health East Clinic showed some weird liver enzymes. She thinks it was from the pre‑workout and told me not to start any new supplements without checking with her first, so if anyone offers to “hook me up” with some new powder at the shop, I’m gonna pass.

Let me know if 10–15 hours sounds doable for you this fall. I really like working here and just want to keep things balanced.

Thanks,
Jordan

Title: Health / mental checklist before college
Date: 2025-11-03

Stuff I don’t want to lose track of once I’m at UW–Madison:

1. Meds/therapy
- Still on sertraline 50 mg from Dr. Werner at UW Health Behavioral Health Clinic. Keep the every‑other‑Tuesday afternoon sessions through at least Dec 2026 like she recommended after the fainting episode at the Westview home game on 9/18/24.
- Before that I was on fluoxetine 10 mg every morning from Dr. Karen Schultz at Madison Family Clinic (since Nov 2023) after I told her about the off‑and‑on self‑harm thoughts when Max was getting evaluated for possible autism at UW Health’s American Family Children’s Hospital and Mom was crying almost every night. Remember how bad that felt = do NOT just stop meds on my own.

2. Anxiety/triggers
- Panic attack during 6th‑hour AP Physics on 09/18/2024 → ended up in the school nurse’s office. Keep using the breathing stuff Ms. Ortiz taught me in Room 214 and don’t blow off counseling if things start to slide.
- IBS flare on the 4/9/25 bus ride to Sun Prairie High (urgent diarrhea, missed warm‑ups). Ms. Ramirez’s hydration/bathroom schedule actually helps on long away trips; adapt that for long drives to meets.

3. Safety/transport
- Don’t ride with Grandpa Robert to games after the 1/12/25 fender‑bender in the Westview gym lot and his early‑stage Parkinson’s diagnosis from SSM Health Janesville.
- Same with Dad driving after beers (esp. after that 8/29/24 scrimmage at Reddan).

4. Supplements
- No random GNC pre‑workouts after the March 2025 liver enzyme scare with Dr. Shah.

Remember: ask for help early instead of waiting until everything explodes.

From: jordan.a.miller06@gmail.com
To: megan.ortiz@westviewhs.edu
Subject: Rides + schedule stuff
Date: 2024-09-03

Hi Ms. Ortiz,

I wanted to give you a heads‑up about a couple things before the season gets busier.

First, rides to away games. Since my dad got that written warning from HR at the plant in Janesville in August after he blew up at his supervisor about overtime, he’s been drinking more. On 8/29/24 he drove me back from a club scrimmage at Reddan Soccer Park after having 3–4 beers. I didn’t say anything in the moment, but I’ve been really nervous about riding with him since. I’m trying to line up rides with teammates’ parents or Mom instead, but if there’s ever a conflict I might need help talking to the coaches.

Second, I’m still kind of on edge about what happened in 6th‑hour AP Physics on 09/18/2024 when I had that panic attack and ended up in the nurse’s office. The every‑other‑Tuesday check‑ins with you in Room 214 have helped a lot, especially with the anxiety about my parents arguing over the gambling debt and maybe having to sell the house on Lakeview Terrace. I just wanted you to know it’s still a thing in the background, especially on test days.

Also, random but related to stress: I’m trying hard to follow the hydration/bathroom schedule we wrote up after the April 9, 2025 Sun Prairie bus incident where I had urgent diarrhea and missed warm‑ups. It’s embarrassing, but it really does make long rides less scary.

Thanks for listening and for letting me keep using your room as a reset spot.

Jordan

Title: Therapy + counseling schedule – fall 2025
Date: 2025-08-20

Recurring events I just added:

Event: Session w/ Dr. Alicia Werner – anxiety/panic follow‑up
When: Every other Tuesday, 3:30–4:15 p.m., starting 2025-09-02
Where: UW Health Behavioral Health Clinic, Madison, WI
Notes: Keep working on panic around games after fainting at Westview home soccer game on 9/18/24. Continue sertraline 50 mg unless she/primary care says otherwise. Plan to stick with this schedule through at least December 2026.

Event: Check‑in w/ Ms. Ortiz
When: Every other Tuesday, 10:30–10:50 a.m. (6th‑hour pass), starting 2025-09-09
Where: Westview High School, Room 214
Notes: Talk about anxiety from AP Physics panic attack on 09/18/2024, parents’ arguments over Dad’s gambling debt, and stress about maybe losing the house on Lakeview Terrace.

Event: Counselor meeting – Ms. Ramirez
When: First Thursday of each month, 8:10–8:25 a.m.
Where: Westview counseling office
Notes: Review sleep, work hours at Lakeside Brew (keep under 15 hrs/week during soccer season; last October at 22–25 hrs I was nodding off in class and AP Physics dropped from A– to C+). Also check in on IBS plan for long bus rides after the 4/9/25 Sun Prairie incident.

Event: Lab check – Dr. Shah
When: 2025-10-15, 4:00 p.m.
Where: UW Health East Clinic
Notes: Follow‑up on abnormal liver enzymes from March 2025 after using GNC pre‑workout for ~6 weeks.

From: jordan.a.miller06@gmail.com
To: carolyn.jensen@example.com
Subject: Quick update on Max (and me)
Date: 2024-03-10

Hi Grandma,

Thanks again for dinner last Sunday and for letting me walk Milo around the block afterward. I wanted to give you a better update on Max and also on how I’ve been doing.

Max is still going through the evaluation for possible autism at UW Health’s American Family Children’s Hospital. The appointments seem to wear him out, but he likes the toys they have there. Mom’s been overwhelmed and crying a lot at night, especially after the longer testing days. I’ve been trying to keep Sophie and Max distracted with soccer in the backyard when I can.

I don’t know if Mom told you, but back in November 2023 I started taking 10 mg of fluoxetine every morning. Dr. Karen Schultz at Madison Family Clinic prescribed it after I told her I’d had some off‑and‑on thoughts about hurting myself last fall when everything with Max’s evaluation was starting and the house felt really tense. The medication has helped level things out, and I’m also doing counseling at school.

I did have a rough moment on 09/18/2024 during 6th‑hour AP Physics at Westview where I had a panic attack and ended up in the nurse’s office. Since then I’ve been meeting with the school counselor, Ms. Ortiz, every other Tuesday in Room 214 to work on anxiety about Mom and Dad’s arguments (especially about Dad’s gambling and money and maybe selling the house on Lakeview Terrace).

I’m okay right now, just juggling school, soccer, and work. It helps knowing you’re nearby in Sun Prairie. Maybe next Sunday I can drive out early and help with groceries again.

Love you,
Jordan

Title: Late‑night brain dump
Date: 2025-04-20

Couldn’t sleep, so writing instead of scrolling.

Still thinking about that January 5, 2024 night with Emily Rogers after her shift at Lakeside Brew. First time having sex and we were idiots and didn’t use a condom. In February 2024 we went to the Planned Parenthood East Side Health Center in Madison for STI screening and emergency contraception for her. All my tests came back negative and everything was okay, but the waiting freaked me out. Need to remember how that felt so I don’t repeat it.

Same with other “almost disasters”: March 2024 at West Towne Mall when Madison Police questioned me in the food court after Tyler James got caught shoplifting a $120 pair of headphones at Best Buy. I didn’t get charged, but Coach Reynolds still put me on a one‑week informal probation from varsity and I missed three days of track. I still feel stupid for even being there.

Then the April 9, 2025 bus ride to Sun Prairie High where I had urgent diarrhea from stress and missed warm‑ups. At least Ms. Ramirez helped me set up that hydration/bathroom schedule so I’m not terrified of every away game now.

And rides: Grandpa Robert rear‑ending that car in the Westview gym parking lot on 1/12/25 and telling Dad and me about his early‑stage Parkinson’s from SSM Health in Janesville; Dad driving us home from Reddan on 8/29/24 after 3–4 beers. I need to trust my gut and say something instead of just sitting there.

Pattern: I wait until after things go bad to act. Goal for college: speak up earlier—about sex, friends, rides, everything.
